intro: Psychotria leilae, also known as "Guayusa", is a native plant species to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been used by indigenous cultures for centuries, and its leaves have been found to possess several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria leilae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant are used to make tea, a popular herbal preparation, which is also known as "Guayusa tea", that has been gaining popularity worldwide due to its potential health benefits and unique flavor profile.
